items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: LeBron James explains how Lakers can beat Thunder

items.headline: Jaylen Brown takes parting shot at Joel Embiid
items.blurb: Jaylen Brown takes parting shot at Joel Embiid — Jaylen Brown took his chance to take one final swipe at Joel Embiid with the Boston Celtics’ season coming to an end. The Celtics star spoke to reporters on Saturday after his team lost Game 7 of their first-round series against Embiid’s Philadelphia 76ers.

items.headline: Why Raptors are playing with house money heading into Game 7
items.blurb: Why Raptors are playing with house money heading into Game 7 — NBA insider Marc Spears joins Jesse Fuchs to preview Game 7 between the Raptors and Cavaliers, discussing the pressure Cleveland is facing, how Scottie Barnes can vault himself into the NBA’s elite, Collin Murray-Boyles impressive series and more!

items.headline: Hawks Unlikely To Make Big Trade This Offseason, Focused On Internal Improvements
items.blurb: Hawks Unlikely To Make Big Trade This Offseason, Focused On Internal Improvements — The Atlanta Hawks are not looking to swing a big trade this offseason, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider. The team is focused on making internal improvements, as well as deciding on the futures of CJ McCollum, Jonathan Kuminga, and Quin Snyder. Hawks execs were encouraged by the team's improvement a

items.headline: Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey Lead 76ers Past Celtics In Game 7 To Complete Historic 3-1 Comeback
items.blurb: Joel Embiid, Tyrese Maxey Lead 76ers Past Celtics In Game 7 To Complete Historic 3-1 Comeback — Joel Embiid scored 34 points with 12 rebounds and six assists Saturday as the Philadelphia 76ers defeated the Boston Celtics 109-100 in Game 7, completing the first comeback from a 3-1 series deficit in franchise history. The Sixers advance to face the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference semifinal

items.blurb: Blazers To Be 'Prime Trade Suitor' For Giannis Antetokounmpo, May Pursue Anthony Davis As Alternative — The Portland Trail Blazers are set to emerge as a "prime trade suitor" for Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, according to Jake Fischer of The People's Insider. The Blazers discussed deals for Antetokounmpo with the Bucks before the trade deadline. The team is looking to build a trade package

items.headline: Anthony Edwards Tells Teammates He Plans To Return During Series Against Spurs
items.blurb: Anthony Edwards Tells Teammates He Plans To Return During Series Against Spurs — Anthony Edwards has informed his Minnesota Timberwolves teammates he intends to return at some point during their second-round series against the San Antonio Spurs, team sources told The Athletic. Edwards has been undergoing around-the-clock treatment on his injured left knee. Chris Finch said Saturday that Edwards' w

items.headline: Five incredible 76ers-Celtics Game 7 stats: Joe Mazzulla's lineup gamble was massive misfire
items.blurb: Five incredible 76ers-Celtics Game 7 stats: Joe Mazzulla's lineup gamble was massive misfire — The Philadelphia 76ers and Boston Celtics played in the first of what we can only hope is three Eastern Conference Game 7 thrillers on Saturday, a 109-100 76ers win.

items.headline: 76ers’ Maxey scores pair of buckets in clutch to ice Game 7 vs. Celtics
items.blurb: 76ers’ Maxey scores pair of buckets in clutch to ice Game 7 vs. Celtics — Watch as Philadelphia 76ers’ Tyrese Maxey scores a pair of buckets in the dying minutes of Game 7 to ice the series win over the Boston Celtics.

items.headline: 76ers complete historic series comeback to stun Celtics in Game 7
items.blurb: 76ers complete historic series comeback to stun Celtics in Game 7 — The Philadelphia 76ers appeared to be heading toward a first-round playoff exit, facing a 3-1 series deficit to the Boston Celtics. With Saturday's 109-100 win in Game 7, they are heading to the Eastern Conference semifinals for the first time since 2023.

items.headline: Franz Wagner Out For Game 7 With Calf Injury
items.blurb: Franz Wagner Out For Game 7 With Calf Injury — The Orlando Magic ruled Franz Wagner out for Game 7 against the Detroit Pistons due to a right calf strain. Wagner will miss his third straight game since suffering the injury in Game 4. With Wagner as the primary defender on Cade Cunningham, Cunningham scored a total of 17 points on 6-of-24 shooting. Without Wagner, Cunningham is averaging 38.5 point

items.headline: 76ers’ Embiid meets Celtics’ Brown at rim for massive block
items.blurb: 76ers’ Embiid meets Celtics’ Brown at rim for massive block — Watch as Philadelphia 76ers’ Joel Embiid gets up high to meet Boston Celtics’ Jaylen Brown at the rim for a massive block in the dying seconds of the second quarter.

items.headline: Michael Porter Jr., Bones Hyland delight in Nuggets' downfall
items.blurb: Michael Porter Jr., Bones Hyland delight in Nuggets' downfall — The Denver Nuggets suffered a disastrous first-round defeat to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who defeated them in six games despite losing their top three guards to injuries.

items.blurb: LeBron James reacts to Raptors star RJ Barrett's game-winner vs. Cavaliers — Shortly before LeBron James and his Los Angeles Lakers squad tipped off Game 6 against the Houston Rockets, RJ Barrett was closing things out in Toronto.

items.blurb: Five burning questions heading into Raptors-Cavaliers Game 7 — The Raptors have earned the right to continue winning basketball games. To get you set for Sunday’s winner-take-all Game 7 against the Cavaliers, Zulfi Sheikh provides five burning questions that could decide the outcome.

items.headline: Is Steve Kerr the right coach to usher out the Stephen Curry era for Warriors?
items.blurb: Is Steve Kerr the right coach to usher out the Stephen Curry era for Warriors? — The organization is trying to get back to its championship ways, and the first question that must be answered this offseason is whether Steve Kerr is the right coach to usher the Warriors out of the Curry era.

items.blurb: Five players or coaches under the most pressure in NBA Game 7s — Plenty is at stake in the NBA's three first-round Game 7s, and not just figuring out who advances to the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.headline: Jayson Tatum Out For Game 7 With Knee Stiffness
items.blurb: Jayson Tatum Out For Game 7 With Knee Stiffness — Jayson Tatum was ruled out of the Boston Celtics' Game 7 against the Philadelphia 76ers on Saturday night due to knee stiffness. "He just came in today with knee discomfort," Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla said during his pregame media availability a few minutes after Tatum was ruled out. "And we decided, the medical staff and myself, that he would not

items.headline: Luka Doncic Has 'No Timeline' For Return, Still Hasn't Advanced To 1-On-1 Or Full Contact Work
items.blurb: Luka Doncic Has 'No Timeline' For Return, Still Hasn't Advanced To 1-On-1 Or Full Contact Work — Los Angeles Lakers guard Luka Doncic still has no timetable for his return from a Grade 2 hamstring strain, according to Cassidy Hubbarth of NBA On Prime. "There's been no timeline, there has been some progress on the court. He's now doing some movement work in drills, getting up shots, but it is contr

items.blurb: Basketball Intelligence For 5/2/26 — The day's best basketball news and analysis.

items.headline: ‘Something about those rims’: LeBron on Barrett’s game-winning shot
items.blurb: ‘Something about those rims’: LeBron on Barrett’s game-winning shot — Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James reacts to RJ Barrett’s incredible game-winning three for the Toronto Raptors in Game 6 of their series against the Cleveland Cavaliers, comparing it to Kawhi Leonard’s iconic shot in 2019.

items.headline: What Magic can learn from collapse in Game 6 vs. Pistons
items.blurb: What Magic can learn from collapse in Game 6 vs. Pistons — One minute into the second half of Game 6, the Orlando Magic had a 24-point lead and were poised to eliminate the top-seeded Detroit Pistons. They proceeded to score the fewest points ever in an NBA playoff half in a 93-79 loss.

items.headline: Charles Barkley has strong opinion about Timberwolves' chances against Spurs
items.blurb: Charles Barkley has strong opinion about Timberwolves' chances against Spurs — Barkley isn't the kind of guy to sugarcoat anything, and he also speaks his mind. So nobody should be surprised that he has a clear vision of the Minnesota Timberwolves' chances in their upcoming playoff series against the San Antonio Spurs.

items.headline: Haliburton draws comparison to Barrett’s game-winning shot for Raptors
items.blurb: Haliburton draws comparison to Barrett’s game-winning shot for Raptors — In this edition of the Central Sixty, Martine Gaillard looks at a fan’s interaction with Philadelphia Flyers defenceman Cam York after his celebratory stick toss, and Tyrese Haliburton’s social media response to RJ Barrett’s game-winning shot.

items.headline: Nikola Jokic Expected To Sign Four-Year, $278M Extension With Nuggets In Offseason
items.blurb: Nikola Jokic Expected To Sign Four-Year, $278M Extension With Nuggets In Offseason — Nikola Jokic admitted a hard reality that the Denver Nuggets are not currently close to title contention after they were eliminated in the first round of the playoffs, but he remains committed to the franchise and is expected to sign a contract extension this offseason. items.blurb: Kevin Durant Burner Question Was Significant Distraction Rockets Never Fully Resolved — Kevin Durant's first season with the Houston Rockets ended in disappointment, marked by a knee injury, a sprained right ankle, and unresolved questions about his relationship with teammates. The Rockets were eliminated by the Los Angeles Lakers in six games with Durant appearing in only of them and his future w

items.headline: Tyrese Maxey: Goes for 30 and 11 in Game 7 win
items.blurb: Tyrese Maxey: Goes for 30 and 11 in Game 7 win — Maxey provided 30 points (11-18 FG, 2-4 3Pt, 6-6 FT), 11 rebounds and seven assists across 45 minutes during Saturday's 109-100 victory over Boston in Game 7 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Thunder, Spurs Heavy Favorites In Western Conference Semis With Lakers, Wolves Facing Key Injuries
items.blurb: Thunder, Spurs Heavy Favorites In Western Conference Semis With Lakers, Wolves Facing Key Injuries — The Western Conference Semifinals are set, with the top-seeded Oklahoma City Thunder hosting No. 4 Los Angeles Los Angeles Lakers, and No. 2 San Antonio Spurs facing No. 6 Minnesota Timberwolves. Both series feature top-10 NBA players sidelined by injury, accentuating the favorite status of the Thu

items.headline: LeBron James Embraces 'Small Victories' As Lakers Eliminate Rockets In Game 6
items.blurb: LeBron James Embraces 'Small Victories' As Lakers Eliminate Rockets In Game 6 — LeBron James made NBA history during the Los Angeles Lakers' 98-78 Game 6 win over Houston Friday, becoming the oldest player to lead all participants in total points scored across a playoff series. James, at the age of 41, finished with 139 points over the six games "It's not my style at all," James told ESPN after po

items.headline: Joel Embiid: Leads Philadelphia to Game 7 win
items.blurb: Joel Embiid: Leads Philadelphia to Game 7 win — Embiid finished with 34 points (12-26 FG, 1-4 3Pt, 9-11 FT), 12 rebounds, six assists and one block across 39 minutes in Saturday's 109-100 win over Boston in Game 7 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jaylen Brown: Drops 33 points in Game 7 loss
items.blurb: Jaylen Brown: Drops 33 points in Game 7 loss — Brown contributed 33 points (12-27 FG, 3-9 3Pt, 6-6 FT), nine rebounds, four assists and three blocks across 40 minutes in Saturday's 109-100 loss to the 76ers in Game 7 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Joel Embiid: Available for Game 7
items.blurb: Joel Embiid: Available for Game 7 — Embiid (abdomen) is available for Game 7 of the Eastern Conference first-round series against Boston, Adam Aaronson of PhillyVoice.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Paul George: Good to go Saturday
items.blurb: Paul George: Good to go Saturday — George (illness) will play in Saturday's Game 7 of the Eastern Conference first-round series against the Celtics, Adam Aaronson of PhillyVoice.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: JJ Redick Says Lakers Winning Series Against Rockets 'A Big Deal'
items.blurb: JJ Redick Says Lakers Winning Series Against Rockets 'A Big Deal' — The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets 98-78 in Game 6 to win the first-round series 4-2 on Friday night. LeBron James finished with 28 points, with Rui Hachimura adding 21 points. Austin Reaves added 15 points in his second game back from an injured oblique. James credited the Lakers' defense with the win. "In order

items.headline: Dunc'd On: ESPN's Zach Kram: Does Fouling Up 3 Even Work?
items.blurb: Dunc'd On: ESPN's Zach Kram: Does Fouling Up 3 Even Work? — Zach Kram of ESPN joins to discuss his recent piece on one of Nate's biggest causes celèbre, fouling up three. He breaks down his reporting on the subject: What do people in the league really think about it? Does the league see this as an issue? And, most importantly, does it even work? items.headline: Hollinger & Duncan: The 2026 Crucible Week 2, Lottery Reform
items.blurb: Hollinger & Duncan: The 2026 Crucible Week 2, Lottery Reform — Hollinger and Duncan react to Minnesota's stunning series win over Denver, the Knicks' historic Game 6 destruction of Atlanta, the proposed lottery reform, and the biggest strategic developments of the playoffs so far. items.headline: Latest update on Luka Doncic doesn't sound good for Lakers
items.blurb: Latest update on Luka Doncic doesn't sound good for Lakers — Luka Doncic does not appear to be anywhere close to returning for the Los Angeles Lakers this postseason.

items.headline: Celtics' Jayson Tatum addresses injury concerns ahead of Game 7
items.blurb: Celtics' Jayson Tatum addresses injury concerns ahead of Game 7 — Given that Tatum is coming off a major injury, it didn't take long before fans sounded the alarms. That's why he wanted to clear the air and ease everyone's minds.

items.headline: ‘Looked like an eternity’: Rajakovic talks wild bounce on Barrett’s winner
items.blurb: ‘Looked like an eternity’: Rajakovic talks wild bounce on Barrett’s winner — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic discusses the team’s 112-110 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 6, touching on RJ Barrett’s miraculous game winner and the effort level from the group in a do-or-die contest.

items.headline: ‘Blessed to have him’: Ja’Kobe Walter pinch hits for Ingram-less Raptors
items.blurb: ‘Blessed to have him’: Ja’Kobe Walter pinch hits for Ingram-less Raptors — Ja’Kobe Walter answered the call, finishing with 24 points, five rebounds, three steals and a block while shooting 7-of-13 from the field and 4-of-9 from three in 43 minutes.

items.headline: Nuggets To Prioritize Retaining Peyton Watson
items.blurb: Nuggets To Prioritize Retaining Peyton Watson — The Denver Nuggets' top priority this offseason will be to retain Peyton Watson, sources told Jake Fischer of The People's Insider. Watson is set to be an unrestricted free agent this summer. It was reported that Watson will draw interest from the Bulls, Lakers, and Nets this offseason. items.headline: Raptors stay alive on RJ Barrett's wild game-winning three
items.blurb: Raptors stay alive on RJ Barrett's wild game-winning three — Seven years ago, Kawhi Leonard sent the Toronto Raptors to the Eastern Conference Finals with a game-winning shot that took a fortunate bounce through the hoop.

items.blurb: Barrett has ‘Kawhi moment’ for hometown Raptors in Game 6 — Danielle Michaud and Michael Grange break down the Toronto Raptors’ 112-110 overtime win over the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 6, discussing RJ Barrett’s “Kawhi moment” with a last-second game winner, and more.
